issues.title: Unified domain model and intake contract migration
issues.description: Finish consolidating the canonical Go task, intake, and workflow vocabulary so downstream runtime slices stop inventing parallel compatibility contracts.

comments.body:
Progress update: restored canonical Go task-contract parity for the remaining `models.py` identity/budget-override gap. `bigclaw-go/internal/domain/task.go` now accepts legacy `task_id` JSON input while preserving the canonical `id` field, emits both identifiers on JSON output for migration compatibility, and carries the Python budget-override fields (`budget_override_actor`, `budget_override_reason`, `budget_override_amount`) on the canonical Go task type. Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/domain/task_test.go` for JSON round-trip behavior and in `bigclaw-go/internal/api/server_test.go` to verify `/tasks` accepts legacy `task_id` payloads.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/domain ./internal/api` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `cee6c12892541a4e485b0338c04965d5ac2d798b`
- `git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `cee6c12892541a4e485b0338c04965d5ac2d798b` (matches local)
- `git ls-remote --heads github symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `cee6c12892541a4e485b0338c04965d5ac2d798b` (matches local)
- `git log -1 --stat` -> `Restore Go task contract parity for legacy task IDs`

Commit: `cee6c12892541a4e485b0338c04965d5ac2d798b`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Progress update: widened the canonical Go billing usage contract to preserve the remaining Python `models.py` metadata shape. `bigclaw-go/internal/billing/statement.go` now uses `map[string]any` for usage metadata instead of narrowing to string-only values, and `bigclaw-go/internal/billing/statement_test.go` now locks round-trip behavior for mixed-type metadata values. Updated `docs/go-domain-intake-parity-matrix.md` to reflect the preserved Python `Dict[str, Any]` billing metadata contract.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/billing` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `82b5bc690477760aa6a3634014042967aa902db8`
- `git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `82b5bc690477760aa6a3634014042967aa902db8` (matches local)
- `git ls-remote --heads github symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `82b5bc690477760aa6a3634014042967aa902db8` (matches local)
- `git log -1 --stat` -> `Preserve Python billing metadata shape in Go`

Commit: `82b5bc690477760aa6a3634014042967aa902db8`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Progress update: restored legacy Python `Task.budget` compatibility on the canonical Go task contract. `bigclaw-go/internal/domain/task.go` now accepts the legacy `budget` JSON field, converts it into canonical `budget_cents` on ingest, and round-trips the legacy `budget` alias alongside `budget_cents` on JSON output so migration-era Python payloads are preserved instead of dropping planned spend. Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/domain/task_test.go` for legacy budget unmarshalling/marshalling and in `bigclaw-go/internal/api/server_test.go` to verify `/tasks` accepts legacy `budget` payloads. Updated `docs/go-domain-intake-parity-matrix.md` and `docs/go-mainline-cutover-issue-pack.md` to record the restored task-budget parity.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/domain ./internal/api` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `614c17dccedbd2cea4adb9d47f4b38b46b17402b`
- `git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `614c17dccedbd2cea4adb9d47f4b38b46b17402b` (matches local)
- `git rev-parse github/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `614c17dccedbd2cea4adb9d47f4b38b46b17402b` (matches local after fetch)
- `git ls-remote --heads github symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `614c17dccedbd2cea4adb9d47f4b38b46b17402b` (matches local)
- `git log -1 --stat` -> `Preserve legacy Python task budget in Go`

Commit: `614c17dccedbd2cea4adb9d47f4b38b46b17402b`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Progress update: normalized legacy Python `Task.state` values at the canonical Go task boundary. `bigclaw-go/internal/domain/task.go` now converts migration-era task states like `Todo`, `In Progress`, `Done`, `Blocked`, and `Failed` into canonical Go runtime states during JSON ingest, so Python-shaped task payloads no longer leak legacy state strings into the Go mainline. Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/domain/task_test.go` for state normalization and in `bigclaw-go/internal/api/server_test.go` to verify `/tasks` accepts legacy state payloads while still queuing new work in the runtime. Updated `docs/go-domain-intake-parity-matrix.md` to record the restored task-state parity.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/domain ./internal/api` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `9a53742ec218addd6ad392ce7d78c6302442daa4`

Commit: `9a53742ec218addd6ad392ce7d78c6302442daa4`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Progress update: aligned the Go workflow model decode path with Python `from_dict` defaults for missing collections. `bigclaw-go/internal/workflow/model.go` now defaults absent workflow lists/maps to empty collections on JSON ingest, including template steps/tags plus run outputs, approval refs, step outputs, and step metadata, so migration-era Python workflow payloads decode into the same empty-collection shape the legacy `models.py` contract produces. Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/workflow/model_test.go` for empty-collection defaults alongside the existing round-trip coverage, and updated `docs/go-domain-intake-parity-matrix.md` to record the restored workflow parity.

Validation:

Commit: `29bbaf5db62c806b64675b9ca8e5616fbe4a68f5`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Progress update: aligned the Go workflow-definition decode path with Python `WorkflowDefinition.from_dict` defaults. `bigclaw-go/internal/workflow/definition.go` now defaults missing step metadata, steps, validation evidence, and approvals to empty collections on JSON ingest, so the Go DSL owner matches the legacy Python shape instead of leaving nil maps/slices in the decoded contract. Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/workflow/definition_test.go` for missing-collection defaults and updated `docs/go-domain-intake-parity-matrix.md` to record the restored DSL-definition parity.

Commit: `8f542dde333b6757643b334660677f4006c3c49c`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Progress update: restored the Python `Task.to_dict()` default output shape on the canonical Go task contract. `bigclaw-go/internal/domain/task.go` now emits the core Python task field set even when values are empty/default, including default `state`, `risk_level`, and `budget` values plus empty list fields for `labels`, `required_tools`, `acceptance_criteria`, and `validation_plan`, while still preserving the canonical Go `id` / `budget_cents` fields and the legacy `task_id` / `budget` aliases. Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/domain/task_test.go` for the Python-style default field set and non-nil collection defaults, and updated `docs/go-domain-intake-parity-matrix.md` to record the restored task JSON output parity.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/domain` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `47136d5947e12277f56eba720017407631212282`
- `git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `47136d5947e12277f56eba720017407631212282` (matches local)

Commit: `47136d5947e12277f56eba720017407631212282`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Progress update: restored the Python `to_dict()` output shape for the remaining workflow, DSL-definition, and billing contract owners. `bigclaw-go/internal/workflow/model.go` now emits the Python field set for templates and runs even when values are empty/default, including default trigger/status values and empty collection fields; `bigclaw-go/internal/workflow/definition.go` now does the same for steps, validation evidence, approvals, and blank template paths; and `bigclaw-go/internal/billing/statement.go` now preserves the Python output shape for usage records and summaries, including empty metadata/rates/usage collections and default numeric totals. Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/workflow/model_test.go`, `bigclaw-go/internal/workflow/definition_test.go`, and `bigclaw-go/internal/billing/statement_test.go`, and updated `docs/go-domain-intake-parity-matrix.md` to record the restored output parity.

Commit: `e58c67fd81fe88951db1cf366b77ef8bb5ec70e2`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Progress update: restored the Python `to_dict()` / `from_dict()` default behavior for the remaining risk and triage contract owners. `bigclaw-go/internal/risk/assessment.go` now preserves the Python field set for assessments and signals, including default low risk level output plus empty signal/mitigation metadata collections; `bigclaw-go/internal/triage/record.go` now preserves the Python field set for triage labels and records, including default `open` status, default `default` queue, label confidence/source defaults, and empty labels/actions collections. Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/risk/assessment_test.go` and `bigclaw-go/internal/triage/record_test.go`, and updated `docs/go-domain-intake-parity-matrix.md` to record the restored risk/triage parity.

Commit: `e8c0da2e983ef9396c1030a7eaae6deeca309831`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Closed `BIG-GOM-301` after finishing the remaining Go-owned domain, intake, workflow, billing, risk, and triage contract parity work.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/domain/task.go` now owns the Python task contract defaults/aliases, including legacy `task_id`, Python `budget`, budget-override fields, legacy task-state normalization, and Python-style default JSON field output.
- `bigclaw-go/internal/risk/assessment.go`, `bigclaw-go/internal/triage/record.go`, `bigclaw-go/internal/workflow/model.go`, `bigclaw-go/internal/workflow/definition.go`, and `bigclaw-go/internal/billing/statement.go` now preserve the Python `to_dict()` / `from_dict()` field sets and default collection behavior for the remaining `models.py` / `dsl.py` surfaces.
- `docs/go-domain-intake-parity-matrix.md`, `docs/go-mainline-cutover-issue-pack.md`, and `docs/parallel-refill-queue.json` now reflect that all `src/bigclaw/models.py`, `connectors.py`, `mapping.py`, and `dsl.py` contract owners for `BIG-GOM-301` have Go owners and the refill queue no longer treats `BIG-GOM-301` as active.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/domain ./internal/intake ./internal/workflow ./internal/risk ./internal/triage ./internal/billing` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `10a232988a3cb11f1a1dc2c5d94df82587e48515`

- `git ls-remote --heads github symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `10a232988a3cb11f1a1dc2c5d94df82587e48515` (matches local)
- `git log -1 --stat` -> `Document BIG-GOM-301 domain parity completion`

Commit: `10a232988a3cb11f1a1dc2c5d94df82587e48515`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

issues.title: Risk, policy, and approval semantics migration
issues.description: Keep porting governance, execution-contract, audit, and policy semantics into Go so review and approval behavior no longer depends on Python-only code paths.

comments.body:
Restored Python risk-scoring parity for invalid budgets in `bigclaw-go/internal/risk/risk.go` by adding a manual-review `budget` factor when `budget_cents` is negative, and added `TestScoreTaskFlagsNegativeBudgetForManualReview` coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/risk/risk_test.go`.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/risk` -> passed
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `69b1d9b4759537af3154cc26999c6f5227c9af80`
- remote SHA check for `origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `69b1d9b4759537af3154cc26999c6f5227c9af80` (matches local)

Commit: `69b1d9b4759537af3154cc26999c6f5227c9af80`
PR URL: unavailable in this workspace mirror (`origin` is a local path, not a GitHub HTTPS remote).

Completed the remaining Go-side audit semantics parity for policy and approval events in `bigclaw-go/internal/observability/*`.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/observability/audit.go` now validates spec-backed execution audit events before JSONL persistence, using top-level `task_id` / `run_id` plus payload fields so malformed approval, takeover, budget-override, and handoff events cannot be written.
- `bigclaw-go/internal/observability/recorder.go` now exposes `RecordSpecEvent`, a spec-aware recorder path that rejects malformed audit events before they mutate in-memory recorder state.
- Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/observability/audit_test.go` and `bigclaw-go/internal/observability/recorder_test.go` for top-level identifier handling, missing required fields, sink rejection, and recorder rejection/acceptance behavior.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/observability` -> passed
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `caecf9d6ce3774d0491d5ddd52e15dc771594236`
- remote SHA check for `origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `caecf9d6ce3774d0491d5ddd52e15dc771594236` (matches local)
- `git log -1 --stat` captured commit `caecf9d6ce3774d0491d5ddd52e15dc771594236` with recorder test coverage; prior supporting commit `1eb6016c8cb1b1add099cdfce166e9dc6f8427e1` enforced spec-backed sink validation.

Commit: `caecf9d6ce3774d0491d5ddd52e15dc771594236`
PR URL: unavailable in this workspace mirror (`origin` is a local path, not a GitHub HTTPS remote).

Synced the completed `BIG-GOM-302` branch to GitHub and refreshed closeout evidence.

What changed:
- pushed `symphony/BIG-GOM-302` to the `github` remote so the completed migration branch exists on GitHub as well as the local mirror
- created PR `BIG-GOM-302: close risk and policy migration slice`
- re-ran the focused Go verification suite for the migrated risk, policy, governance, contract, observability, and API surfaces

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/risk ./internal/policy ./internal/governance ./internal/contract ./internal/observability ./internal/api` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `b05db848be9eec330245fd92a8a6c895ed36c4a1`
- `git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `b05db848be9eec330245fd92a8a6c895ed36c4a1` (matches local)
- `git ls-remote --heads github symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `b05db848be9eec330245fd92a8a6c895ed36c4a1` (matches local)

PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

issues.title: Workflow orchestration and scheduler loop migration
issues.description: Close the Go execution loop with workflow-aware orchestration, journal state, and runtime closeout so active development no longer needs the Python workflow engine.

comments.body:
Improved the Go orchestrator loop in `bigclaw-go/internal/orchestrator/loop.go` so it no longer waits a full poll interval before touching queued work, drains available work within a single cycle, and falls back to a safe default poll interval when unset. Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/orchestrator/loop_test.go` for immediate startup, per-cycle draining, and default poll interval behavior.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/orchestrator` -> passed
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/orchestrator ./internal/worker ./cmd/bigclawd` -> passed (`cmd/bigclawd` has no test files)
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `b7544e538d2ed7ef8a4a2461df0b4d709c77d9c3`
- `git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `b7544e538d2ed7ef8a4a2461df0b4d709c77d9c3` (matches local)
- `git ls-remote --heads github symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `b7544e538d2ed7ef8a4a2461df0b4d709c77d9c3` (matches local)
- `git log -1 --stat` -> `Drain scheduler work immediately in orchestrator loop`

Commit: `b7544e538d2ed7ef8a4a2461df0b4d709c77d9c3`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Hardened the Go worker runtime defaults for the active orchestration slice.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/worker/runtime.go` now falls back to sane default lease and task timeout values when `LeaseTTL` or `TaskTimeout` are unset, preventing zero-value heartbeat ticker panics and immediate timeout cancellations in unattended bootstrap/runtime paths.
- The runtime now uses those effective defaults consistently for `LeaseNext`, heartbeat renewals, cancellation polling, and execution deadlines.
- Added `TestRuntimeUsesDefaultTimeoutAndLeaseWhenUnset` in `bigclaw-go/internal/worker/runtime_test.go` to lock the zero-value runtime behavior.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/worker` -> passed
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `a3a43f671feb3ab33f3fa9da7faa046e846ecf8c`
- `git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `a3a43f671feb3ab33f3fa9da7faa046e846ecf8c` (matches local)
- `git ls-remote --heads github symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `a3a43f671feb3ab33f3fa9da7faa046e846ecf8c` (matches local)
- `git log -1 --stat` -> `Default worker runtime lease and timeout settings`

Commit: `a3a43f671feb3ab33f3fa9da7faa046e846ecf8c`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Finished the remaining zero-value dependency hardening for the active orchestration runtime slice.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/worker/runtime.go` now falls back to `scheduler.New()` when the runtime scheduler is unset and to an empty `executor.NewRegistry()` when the runtime registry is unset, preventing nil-pointer crashes in unattended runtime/bootstrap paths.
- The nil registry path now dead-letters cleanly through the existing executor-missing behavior instead of panicking.
- Added `TestRuntimeDefaultsNilSchedulerAndRegistryToSafeBehavior` in `bigclaw-go/internal/worker/runtime_test.go` to prove the zero-value runtime path stays safe.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/worker` -> passed
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `d9f938e6d92822fcf46a12eb86d44e45410cc83e`
- `git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `d9f938e6d92822fcf46a12eb86d44e45410cc83e` (matches local)

- `git log -1 --stat` -> `Default nil worker runtime dependencies`

Commit: `d9f938e6d92822fcf46a12eb86d44e45410cc83e`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Added the last obvious zero-value queue guard for the active orchestration runtime slice.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/worker/runtime.go` now returns a clean idle/no-op result when the runtime queue is unset instead of panicking on `LeaseNext`.
- Added `TestRuntimeSkipsCleanlyWhenQueueUnset` in `bigclaw-go/internal/worker/runtime_test.go` to verify the zero-value queue path stays safe.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/worker` -> passed
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `1776246156301eb23b995b1b046c05b4bfac347c`

- `git log -1 --stat` -> `Guard worker runtime when queue is unset`

Commit: `1776246156301eb23b995b1b046c05b4bfac347c`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Fixed Go worker runtime closeout state persistence for the active orchestration slice.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/worker/runtime.go` now stores final task snapshots into the recorder for success, dead-letter, and retry paths instead of leaving the last stored task state at `running`.
- Added assertions in `bigclaw-go/internal/worker/runtime_test.go` so recorder-backed task snapshots now prove `succeeded`, `dead_letter`, and `retrying` closeout states.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/worker` -> passed
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `c6313c020484f0a807a4ece58b8ea2a1674f1804`
- `git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `c6313c020484f0a807a4ece58b8ea2a1674f1804` (matches local)

Aligned workflow journal closeout status with acceptance outcomes for the active orchestration slice.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/workflow/engine.go` now records the `closeout` journal step as `complete` only for accepted runs and otherwise mirrors the acceptance status (`rejected` / `needs-approval`) instead of always claiming closeout completed.
- Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/workflow/engine_test.go` for rejected-evidence and pending-approval closeout journal cases.

Validation:

- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` -> passed

Closed `BIG-GOM-303` after finishing the remaining Go orchestration/runtime hardening slice.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/orchestrator/loop.go` now runs queued work immediately on startup, drains work within a single cycle, and uses a safe default poll interval when unset.
- `bigclaw-go/internal/worker/runtime.go` now hardens zero-value runtime construction (`LeaseTTL`, `TaskTimeout`, scheduler, registry, queue) and persists final recorder task states for success, retry, and dead-letter closeout.
- `bigclaw-go/internal/workflow/engine.go` now records workflow `closeout` journal status in line with acceptance outcomes instead of always reporting `complete`.
- Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/orchestrator/loop_test.go`, `bigclaw-go/internal/worker/runtime_test.go`, and `bigclaw-go/internal/workflow/engine_test.go` for immediate drain behavior, zero-value runtime safety, recorder closeout state persistence, and workflow closeout journal accuracy.

Added direct package coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/reporting/reporting_test.go` and tightened `bigclaw-go/internal/api/expansion_test.go` so the `/v2/reports/weekly/export` surface proves the richer markdown is emitted through the API.

issues.title: Control center, triage, and operations view migration
issues.description: Finish moving operator-facing status, control-center, and triage endpoints to Go once the workflow runtime and reporting surfaces are stable.

comments.body:
Fixed the eng-lead home takeover card to use active control-center takeovers instead of reusing the blocked-run count.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/product/console.go` now accepts an explicit active-takeover signal for role homes and uses it for the `takeovers` card.
- `bigclaw-go/internal/api/expansion.go` now computes that signal from `Control.ActiveTakeovers()` when serving `/v2/home`.
- Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/product/console_test.go` and `bigclaw-go/internal/api/expansion_test.go` to prove blocked runs without takeovers do not inflate the home card.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/product ./internal/api` -> passed
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `9d05785805be3869362b53031f195dd407f01378`
- `git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `9d05785805be3869362b53031f195dd407f01378` (matches local)
- `git ls-remote --heads github symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `9d05785805be3869362b53031f195dd407f01378` (matches local)
- `git log -1 --stat` -> `Fix home takeover counts for eng lead`

Commit: `9d05785805be3869362b53031f195dd407f01378`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Stopped `/v2/home` and `/v2/navigation` from letting `?role=` override an explicit viewer role.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/api/expansion.go` now resolves the effective home/navigation role from the explicit viewer role headers/query first, only falling back to `?role=` when no viewer role was supplied.
- The `?role=` path is now normalized through the control-role mapping instead of accepting raw strings.
- Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/api/expansion_test.go` proving explicit `X-BigClaw-Role` headers prevent role escalation on both endpoints.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/api` -> passed
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `dfd4a7b756bfec520f577896df6fc2796068f4d5`
- `git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `dfd4a7b756bfec520f577896df6fc2796068f4d5` (matches local)
- `git ls-remote --heads github symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `dfd4a7b756bfec520f577896df6fc2796068f4d5` (matches local)
- `git log -1 --stat` -> `Honor explicit viewer role on home endpoints`

Commit: `dfd4a7b756bfec520f577896df6fc2796068f4d5`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Scoped eng-lead home cards to the authenticated viewer team so home counts match the linked team-scoped dashboards and control-center views.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/api/expansion.go` now derives the home task snapshot from the viewer team when the caller is team-scoped and filters active takeover counts through the same team scope.
- `/v2/home` now reuses `filteredTasks` and `filteredActiveTakeovers` instead of aggregating the entire recorder/control snapshot for eng-lead viewers.
- Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/api/expansion_test.go` proving blocked and takeover counts only include the authenticated team.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/api` -> passed
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...` -> passed
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `627c27f9cd61ac95d273e3da3f6d0b6943945ec3`
- `git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `627c27f9cd61ac95d273e3da3f6d0b6943945ec3` (matches local)
- `git ls-remote --heads github symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `627c27f9cd61ac95d273e3da3f6d0b6943945ec3` (matches local)
- `git log -1 --stat` -> `Scope eng lead home cards to viewer team`

Commit: `627c27f9cd61ac95d273e3da3f6d0b6943945ec3`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Filtered console navigation by explicit operator-role permissions derived from the legacy execution contract so queue, flow, regression, and billing links stop leaking across roles.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/product/console.go` now annotates restricted nav items with explicit roles and exposes `NavigationForRole` to filter sections/items by effective role.
- `bigclaw-go/internal/api/expansion.go` now serves `/v2/navigation` from `NavigationForRole(role)` instead of returning the full nav tree to every caller.
- Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/product/navigation_test.go` and tightened `bigclaw-go/internal/api/expansion_test.go` so eng leads lose billing/flow links and VP Eng loses queue-control links while keeping permitted read surfaces.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/product ./internal/api` -> passed

PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Closed `BIG-GOM-305` after tightening the remaining Go-owned operator-view mismatches on home and navigation surfaces.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/api/expansion.go` now serves `/v2/home` and `/v2/navigation` from the authenticated viewer role/team instead of trusting global snapshots or query-param role overrides.
- `bigclaw-go/internal/product/console.go` now uses active takeover counts for eng-lead home cards and filters restricted navigation items by explicit operator-role permissions derived from the legacy execution contract.
- Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/product/console_test.go`, `bigclaw-go/internal/product/navigation_test.go`, and `bigclaw-go/internal/api/expansion_test.go` for takeover counts, team-scoped home cards, explicit-role precedence, and role-filtered navigation.

issues.title: Workflow, bootstrap, and GitHub sync toolchain migration
issues.description: Keep hardening the Go-first bootstrap, refill, and GitHub sync toolchain so unattended multi-issue work no longer relies on Python entrypoints or Linear-only operators.

comments.body:
Added Go-owned local tracker comment support for the active tooling slice.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/refill/local_store.go` now appends structured comments into `local-issues.json` while preserving existing comments and refreshing `updated_at`.
- `bigclaw-go/cmd/bigclawctl/main.go` now exposes `bigclawctl issue comment` and `bigclawctl issue state` for local tracker updates without falling back to Python helpers.
- Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/refill/local_store_test.go` and `bigclaw-go/cmd/bigclawctl/main_test.go` for comment persistence and CLI behavior.

Validation:
- `cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/refill ./cmd/bigclawctl` -> passed

PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

Reduced local tracker churn for Go-owned issue updates.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/refill/local_store.go` now decodes local issues/comments into ordered typed records, preserves unknown fields, disables HTML escaping, and avoids serializing absent `comments` arrays.
- Local tracker updates now keep operator evidence like `&&` and `->` readable instead of rewriting them as `\u0026\u0026` / `\u003e`.
- Added `TestLocalIssueStoreAppendIssueCommentDoesNotEscapeBodyText` in `bigclaw-go/internal/refill/local_store_test.go` to lock the literal-text behavior.

Validation:

- `git ls-remote --heads github symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `a0cd161791c68b5fd9fddd3e27b7eec7e918fe4a` (pre-sync)

- `bigclaw-go/cmd/bigclawctl/main.go` now exposes `bigclawctl issue create` and `bigclawctl issue list` alongside the existing state/comment flows.
- Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/refill/local_store_test.go` and `bigclaw-go/cmd/bigclawctl/main_test.go`, and updated `README.md` plus `docs/parallel-refill-queue.md` to point operators at the Go-native tracker CLI.

issues.title: Python deprecation and Go-only mainline switch
issues.description: Freeze or retire the remaining Python runtime entrypoints only after the Go workflow, observability, and repo collaboration surfaces have matching owners and validation.

comments.body:

Added focused coverage in `tests/test_legacy_entrypoint.py` to lock the deprecation notice text and parser metadata.

issues.title: Broaden validation continuation gate refresh beyond run_all
issues.description: Extend the repo-native validation continuation scorecard/policy-gate refresh into the broader workflow/orchestration entrypoint so continuation evidence stays current outside direct run_all.sh execution.

comments.body:
Closed `BIG-PAR-088` after moving validation-bundle continuation refresh into the reusable bundle exporter instead of relying on `run_all.sh` alone.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/scripts/e2e/export_validation_bundle.py` now owns continuation refresh in `auto|always|off` modes, regenerates the continuation scorecard and policy gate when the shared-queue companion proof is present, and records the refresh result in the exported summary JSON.
- `bigclaw-go/scripts/e2e/validation_bundle_continuation_scorecard.py` and `bigclaw-go/scripts/e2e/validation_bundle_continuation_policy_gate.py` now accept `--repo-root` so the exporter can run them against any target checkout/layout instead of only the current repo path.
- `tests/test_parallel_validation_bundle.py` now exercises direct bundle export with continuation refresh enabled, and `bigclaw-go/docs/e2e-validation.md` documents that direct bundle export workflows refresh continuation artifacts too.

Validation:
- `python3 -m py_compile bigclaw-go/scripts/e2e/export_validation_bundle.py bigclaw-go/scripts/e2e/validation_bundle_continuation_scorecard.py bigclaw-go/scripts/e2e/validation_bundle_continuation_policy_gate.py` -> passed
- `python3 - <<"PY" ... direct temporary-repo export_validation_bundle harness ...` -> passed (`continuation.refreshed=True`, `policy_gate_recommendation=hold`, `scorecard.summary.latest_run_id=20260315T120000Z`)
- `python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_parallel_validation_bundle.py -vv` -> environment failure in this workspace (`exit code -1` with no emitted output), so the direct harness above was used as the executable verification path
- `git rev-parse HEAD` -> `c12e8d8c3f2dd5f4c896a301fb6b986c19eb2860`
- `git rev-parse origin/symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `c12e8d8c3f2dd5f4c896a301fb6b986c19eb2860` (matches local)
- `git ls-remote --heads github symphony/BIG-GOM-302` -> `c12e8d8c3f2dd5f4c896a301fb6b986c19eb2860` (matches local)
- `git log -1 --stat` -> `Refresh continuation artifacts from bundle export`

Commit: `c12e8d8c3f2dd5f4c896a301fb6b986c19eb2860`
PR URL: https://github.com/OpenAGIs/BigClaw/pull/138

issues.title: Co-locate shared-queue proof with validation bundle lineage
issues.description: Reduce the standalone-proof caveat by threading shared-queue coordination evidence into the same validation bundle lineage metadata used by the continuation scorecard and policy gate.
